name: EpistemicTagVocab
description: Controlled uco-core:tag values for CASE/UCO CTI and investigation graphs that separate source fidelity, claim stance, and digest availability without inventing facts.
note: These are recommended string tags, not OWL individuals. Graphs remain valid if they use other tags; critics and recipes treat members of this vocabulary as first-class signals.

epistemic_stance.description: How strongly the source supports the claim as a world-state fact.

members.description: Directly observed or repeatedly executed behavior attributed by the source.
Stated by the report/vendor but not independently re-observed by the modeler.
Code/capability path or conditional functionality, not necessarily executed in the reported incident.
Analyst or vendor assessment about nature/motive/classification.
Explicit hypothesis; pair with verbal confidence tags when available.
Modeler enrichment (e.g. ATT&CK mapping) not asserted by the original vendor prose.
Activity cluster without a justified named Organization performer.
Source text contains an internal contradiction that must be preserved, not resolved.

confidence_verbal.description: Verbal confidence when the source does not publish a numeric score.

members.description: Low verbal confidence from the source or modeler.
High verbal confidence without a numeric facet.

hash_and_bytes.description: Digest / byte-availability status for critic severity and provenance honesty.

members.description: Source does not publish a digest; do not invent one. Critic may emit medium CRIT-H-DERIVED-NO-HASH.
Bytes were never acquired by the investigation (CTI/report-only).
Object is known to exist but payload/content is intentionally omitted from the graph.

mapping_provenance.description: How ATT&CK or other framework mappings were produced.

members.description: Node carries an ATT&CK technique mapping.
Mapping authored by the CASE/UCO exemplar modeler, not vendor-asserted.
Technique ID did not exist (or was not public) at the report date.
